title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: Dirty Oil Flows Libya to European destinations
Higher growth rates of dirty Libyan exports to Italy, Spain, France & the Netherlands in 2Q - 3Q 2023
The third week of September began on a positive note for VLCC crude oil freight rates, moving well off the August lows and setting a more energetic tone in the transition from the summer season. It remains uncertain whether this newfound momentum will continue through the end of the month as the market hopes for a stronger recovery during the upcoming winter period. Downward pressure continues to be observed in the Aframax segment.

children.title: Chart of the Week: Dirty Oil Flows Libya to European destinations
section_path: Chart of the Week: Dirty Oil Flows Libya to European destinations
children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week: Dirty Oil Flows Libya to European destinations
children.summary:
Higher growth rates of dirty Libyan exports to Italy, Spain, France & the Netherlands in 2Q - 3Q 2023
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-21T205048.187.png | assets/2023-09-22_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-21t205048187_d7eba43ca1da.png
The third week of September began on a positive note for VLCC crude oil freight rates, moving well off the August lows and setting a more energetic tone in the transition from the summer season. It remains uncertain whether this newfound momentum will continue through the end of the month as the market hopes for a stronger recovery during the upcoming winter period.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT
children.summary:
Market Rates (WS)
ŌĆśDirty' WS VLCC - Suezmax - Aframax Weaker
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-21T205315.982.png | assets/2023-09-22_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-21t205315982_9d564de2fd4e.png
Sentiment seems to have been revised upward for the VLCC MEG-China route, whereas, there is still downward pressure in the Aframax. VLCC MEG-China freight rates rose rose 18% from the previous week, WS 42, the first sign of an upturn since late June.

children.title: ŌĆśProduct' WS LR2 Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-21T205444.021.png | assets/2023-09-22_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-21t205444021_9bb96287ed72.png
LR2 AG freight rates fell to 135 WS, after exceeding 140 WS last week, while a flatter trend appears to be emerging for the coming days.

children.title: Panamax Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-21T205645.016.png | assets/2023-09-22_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-21t205645016_ca42786a6200.png
Panamax Carib-to-USG rates fell to 132 WS, down 65% from a year ago, with the downward trend continuing since the end of Week 27.

children.title: ŌĆśClean' MR Steady
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-21T205740.830.png | assets/2023-09-22_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-21t205740830_20872f7e0c62.png
MR1 rates for the Baltic continent remained at the previous week's level of about WS 183, while the upward trend seems to be flattening. MR2 rates for shipments from the continent to the U.S.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
'Dirty' (#vessels) - Mixed
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-21T205912.988.png | assets/2023-09-22_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-21t205912988_1487b6ef9d0b.png
Crude oil tanker supply continued to develop unevenly in the third week of September, however, in the VLCC segment, the number hovers below the annual average. VLCC Ras Tanura: The current number of ships is 56, almost 17 below the annual average, and it remains to be seen whether the number will continue at a low level.

children.title: 'Clean' LR2 (#vessels) - Increasing
children.summary: Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-21T210156.880.png | assets/2023-09-22_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-21t210156880_30190800ab44.png

children.title: MR1 (#vessels) - Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-21T210205.993.png | assets/2023-09-22_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-21t210205993_beb38a13763f.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The number of ships recorded a sudden increase in the third week of September, reaching 8 ships, 4 above the annual average. Clean MR1 Algeria Skikda: The current count is 24 ships, surprisingly below the week 35 peak (~40), while the trend over the last three weeks continues to be below the annual average.

children.title: ŌĆś Dirty ' Increasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-09-21T210346.787.png | assets/2023-09-22_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-21t210346787_18ae1704cc58.png
Dirty tonne days: In the third week of September, the percentage increase in demand (tonne days) for all crude oil vessel size categories held decreasing pace, although there were signs of an upturn in Aframax

children.title: ŌĆśClean' Panamax / MR Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: assets/2023-09-22_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-09-21t210435459_ed4ca06c3e31.png
Panamax tonne days: Downward pressure is maintained in the third week of September, with the last peak recorded in week 36. Clean MR tonne days: The downward pressure is revised significantly downward for size MR1, while the demand sentiment for MR2 seems to be relatively stable.
